gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP window collapse via low pool observation cardinality enables sandwich attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX falls back to oldestObservation when pool cardinality=1, collapsing the 900s TWAP window to ~12s (1 block), making amountOutMinimum near-zero after a single-block price manipulation
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild with near-zero slippage protection, extracting up to ~91% of the TitanX-denominated value from the protocol's build allocation

live_preconditions.name: TORUS/TITAN_X pool oldestObservationSecondsAgo < 900 after attacker manipulation block
live_preconditions.observed_value: unknown — depends on cardinality and last swap timestamp
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call <pool_address> 'observe(uint32[])(int56[],uint160[])' '[900]' --rpc-url $ETH_RPC_URL 2>&1 | grep -q revert && echo 'cardinality too low, attack viable'

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Sets secondsAgo = min(900, oldestObservation). With cardinality=1 and a recent manipulation swap, oldestObservation ~12, collapsing TWAP window to 12 seconds — effectively a spot price from the manipulated block.
amountOutMinimum = manipulated_quote * 0.9. If quote is near-zero due to TWAP collapse, minimum output is near-zero, providing no sandwich protection.
Public nonReentrant entry point that calls _swapTitanXForTorus with protocol-owned TitanX. Any caller can trigger this once per interval, making it a predictable MEV target.
Returns block.timestamp - observationTimestamp of the oldest stored observation. With cardinality=1, this equals time since last swap, not a true historical window.
require(secondsAgo != 0) is the only guard. Attacker manipulates in block N so oldestObservation >= 12 in block N+1, bypassing this check.

attacker_inputs: Block N: large swap in TORUS/TITAN_X Uniswap V3 pool (Torus→TitanX direction) to push tick to very low Torus price, writing a new observation at block.timestamp(N)
Block N+1: call swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild(deadline) or sandwich the legitimate caller's pending transaction
call_sequence: Block N: attacker.swap(pool, Torus→TitanX, largeAmount) — manipulates tick, writes observation at T_N
Block N+1 front-run: attacker.swap(pool, TitanX→Torus, amount) — pushes price further against protocol
Block N+1 victim tx: swapTitanXForTorusAndBuild(deadline) — getOldestObservationSecondsAgo returns ~12, secondsAgo=12, quote near-zero, amountOutMinimum near-zero, swap executes at manipulated price
Block N+1 back-run: attacker.swap(pool, Torus→TitanX, amount) — captures sandwich profit
state_after: protocol received near-zero Torus for half the interval TitanX
attacker captured price impact delta as MEV profit
totalTitanXBuilt and titanXUsedForBuilds incremented by full titanXToUse amount

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: MEV sandwich profit: difference between fair Torus output and near-zero actual output, captured as price impact in the TORUS/TITAN_X pool. With 10x price manipulation and 10% slippage tolerance, attacker can extract up to ~91% of the fair Torus value of the swapped TitanX.
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol spends half the interval TitanX allocation (half of titanXToUse) but receives near-zero Torus, resulting in severely undercollateralized liquidity addition. The remainder TitanX is also added as liquidity paired with near-zero Torus, further diluting the LP position.
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is not entitled to any portion of the protocol's TitanX build allocation. The TWAP mechanism was explicitly designed to prevent this extraction. The fallback to a 12-second window defeats the protection entirely when pool cardinality is 1.

setup: Confirm titanXIntervalsBuild[lastTitanXBuildIntervalNumber].amountBurned == 0 or advance time to create new interval
Fund attacker with sufficient TitanX (whale impersonation or deal())
transaction_sequence: vm.roll(block.number); pool.swap(attacker, false, largeAmount, MIN_SQRT_RATIO+1, '') — push tick to minimum, write observation at T_N
uint256 manipulatedQuote = buyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX(half); assertLt(manipulatedQuote, fairQuote / 5, 'quote not manipulated')
expected_assertions: assertLt(torusPurchased, fairQuote * 90 / 100, 'sandwich extracted value: torusPurchased below 90% of fair quote')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If pool observationCardinality > 1 AND getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(pool) >= 900 at the time of the call, the TWAP window does not collapse and the finding should be rejected. Also reject if torusPurchased >= fairQuote * 90 / 100.
